files.content:
---
title: Footnote
docs:
  - route: /docs/markdown
    title: Markdown
---

<ComponentPreview name="footnote-demo" />

The preview demonstrates three states:

- a resolved reference with live hover content
- an unresolved reference with no matching definition yet plus a one-click
  definition repair
- a duplicate-definition identifier that surfaces a warning
- typing `[^` in the default editor kit opens an inline footnote combobox

<PackageInfo>

## Features

- Parse GFM footnote references and definitions into dedicated Plate nodes
- Serialize dedicated footnote nodes back to real footnote markdown
- Insert footnote references and definitions with one transform
- Trigger footnote insertion inline with a `[^` combobox in the default UI kit
- Recreate a missing definition from an unresolved reference without inserting a
  duplicate reference
- Keep the first duplicate definition canonical and offer explicit renumber
  repair for later duplicates
- Navigate between references and definitions with package helpers

</PackageInfo>

## Kit Usage

<Steps>

### Installation

The fastest way to add footnote-aware markdown is with the `MarkdownKit`, which includes `MarkdownPlugin` plus the footnote plugins for the default markdown profile and works with [Plate UI](/docs/installation/plate-ui).

<ComponentSource name="markdown-kit" />

### Add Kit

```tsx
import { createPlateEditor } from 'platejs/react';
import { MarkdownKit } from '@/components/editor/plugins/markdown-kit';

const editor = createPlateEditor({
  plugins: [
    // ...otherPlugins,
    ...MarkdownKit,
  ],
});
```

</Steps>

## Manual Usage

<Steps>

### Installation

```bash
npm install @platejs/footnote @platejs/markdown remark-gfm
```

### Add Plugins

```tsx
import {
  FootnoteDefinitionPlugin,
  FootnoteReferencePlugin,
} from '@platejs/footnote/react';
import { MarkdownPlugin } from '@platejs/markdown';
import { createPlateEditor } from 'platejs/react';
import remarkGfm from 'remark-gfm';

const editor = createPlateEditor({
  plugins: [
    // ...otherPlugins,
    FootnoteReferencePlugin,
    FootnoteDefinitionPlugin,
    MarkdownPlugin.configure({
      options: {
        remarkPlugins: [remarkGfm],
      },
    }),
  ],
});
```

### Configure Plugins

Footnote plugins are usually paired with `MarkdownPlugin` and `remark-gfm` so
markdown like `[^1]` and `[^1]: Footnote text` round-trips as dedicated
footnote nodes instead of plain fallback text.

Current package surface:

- `tf.insert.footnote`
- `api.footnote.nextId`
- `api.footnote.definition`
- `api.footnote.definitions`
- `api.footnote.definitionText`
- `api.footnote.duplicateDefinitions`
- `api.footnote.isResolved`
- `api.footnote.hasDuplicateDefinitions`
- `api.footnote.duplicateIdentifiers`
- `api.footnote.isDuplicateDefinition`
- `api.footnote.references`
- `tf.footnote.focusDefinition`
- `tf.footnote.focusReference`
- `tf.footnote.createDefinition`
- `tf.footnote.normalizeDuplicateDefinition`

The package owns node semantics, insert behavior, identifier allocation, and
navigation helpers.

App-level surfaces like hover-card preview, inline `[^` combobox insertion,
slash-command entries, and toolbar integration are built on top of those
package helpers.

Lookup behavior is normalized:

- definition content is the single source of truth
- references do not store copied preview text
- `definition`, `definitionText`, `references`, and `nextId` read through a
  lazy per-editor registry instead of rescanning the whole document on every
  render
- `definitions`, `isResolved`, `hasDuplicateDefinitions`, and
  `duplicateIdentifiers` expose resolution state for hover, navigation, and
  diagnostics
- unresolved references can create their missing definition from the reference
  surface without inserting another reference
- later duplicate definitions are treated as invalid siblings instead of
  pretending they are equal canonical targets
- explicit repair can renumber a later duplicate to the next free identifier

That keeps hover preview and navigation cheap even when one definition has many
references.

If you want custom rendering, assign your own components:

```tsx
import {
  FootnoteDefinitionPlugin,
  FootnoteReferencePlugin,
} from '@platejs/footnote/react';
import { createPlateEditor } from 'platejs/react';

const editor = createPlateEditor({
  plugins: [
    FootnoteReferencePlugin.withComponent(MyFootnoteReference),
    FootnoteDefinitionPlugin.withComponent(MyFootnoteDefinition),
  ],
});
```

- `withComponent`: Assigns your own React component to render footnote
  references or definitions.

</Steps>

## Plugins

### `FootnoteReferencePlugin`

Plugin for inline footnote reference nodes.

### `FootnoteDefinitionPlugin`

Plugin for block footnote definition nodes.

## Transforms

### `tf.insert.footnote`

Insert a footnote reference at the current selection, create the matching
definition if it does not exist yet, and move the caret into the definition
body.

Default behavior:

- allocate the next free numeric identifier
- replace the current selection with the reference
- seed the definition body with the selected fragment when the selection is
  expanded
- avoid duplicate definitions when the identifier already exists
- accept `focusDefinition: false` to keep the caret inline after the inserted
  reference

### `tf.footnote.normalizeDuplicateDefinition`

Renumber a later duplicate definition so the first definition with the shared
identifier remains canonical.

### `tf.footnote.createDefinition`

Create the missing definition for an existing identifier and focus its body
without inserting another reference.

### `tf.footnote.focusDefinition`

Jump the selection into the matching footnote definition body.

### `tf.footnote.focusReference`

Jump the selection to the matching footnote reference.
